content.text:
<user_query>
Install a VSIX extension on Windows. The file path is: d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix

Steps:
1. Verify the file exists
2. Find Cursor CLI - common paths: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd
3. Run: cursor --install-extension "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"
   Or use full path to cursor.cmd if not on PATH
4. If cursor fails, try code CLI similarly
5. Return the full command output and whether installation succeeded
</user_query>

Verifying the VSIX file and locating the Cursor CLI.

[REDACTED]

input.command: Test-Path -LiteralPath "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"; if (Test-Path -LiteralPath "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix") { Get-Item -LiteralPath "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix" | Select-Object FullName, Length, LastWriteTime }
input.description: Verify VSIX file exists and show details

input.command:
$paths = @(
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd"
); foreach ($p in $paths) { Write-Output "Exists($p): $(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p)" }; Get-Command cursor -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Source; Get-Command code -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Source
input.description: Find cursor.cmd and code CLI paths

content.text:
Re-running with full permissions so we can access D: and install the extension.

input.description: Verify VSIX and cursor.cmd with full access
Test cmd output and list VSIX

input.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "Write-Output 'test'; Test-Path 'd:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix' | Out-String" 2>&1 | Out-File -FilePath "$env:TEMP\vsix-install-log.txt" -Encoding utf8; Get-Content "$env:TEMP\vsix-install-log.txt"
input.description: Write command output to temp log file

input.command: cd C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window && echo test > install-test.txt && type install-test.txt

input.description: Check if test file was created
Run VSIX install and write log to workspace
